RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL DATE AND RE-OPEN EXPERT DISCOVERY

No objections made and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion to continue the trial date and re-open expert discovery for the limited purpose of replacing plaintiffs' expert Dr. Herman Bruch is granted in part and denied in part. The trial date, currently set for July 6, 2015 at 11:15a.m. in Department 503, is maintained. That is the date plaintiffs have requested to be the trial date and thus there will be no trial continuance. Expert discovery is re-opened solely to allow the following: plaintiffs may designate an expert in place of Dr. Bruch and such expert may prepare a report and be presented for deposition; if needed, defendants may supplement their expert witness designation limited to responding to plaintiffs' new designation. Discovery is re-opened to allow any party to obtain plaintiff's updated medical records. This re-opened discovery closes on the new trial date. All other expert and fact discovery remains closed. No opposition filed. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)

RULING:
DEFENDANT SPX COOLING TECHNOLOGIES, INC. FKA MARLEY COOLING TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF ORDER GRANTING PLAINTIFFS MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT

Hearing held. The Court ruled as follows: Defendant SPX Cooling Technologies, Inc.'s motion for reconsideration of order granting plaintiffs' motion for leave to file first amended complaint is granted. Plaintiffs' objection is overruled. Defendant's motion is timely as the 10-day period within which to file such motion was extended by two court days due to plaintiffs electronically serving and filing the notice of entry of order, (CCP 1008(a), 1010.6(a)(4)). Since plaintiffs' objection is overruled and no substantive opposition was filed, defendant's motion is granted because it is unopposed. The Court reconsiders plaintiffs' motion for leave to file first amended complaint, vacates its November 26, 2014 ruling on that motion and now denies plaintiffs' motion for leave to amend. Plaintiffs do not allege a claim for punitive damages against SPX Cooling Technologies, Inc. Plaintiffs request regarding sanctions is denied. Trial date of July 6, 2015 remains. Discovery remains closed. Counsel for defendant to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Teri L. RULING:
DEFENDANT CLEAVER-BROOKS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Cleaver-Brooks, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Shaiffer was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar because it did not demonstrate that plaintiffs failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive interrogatories designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiffs have to support their contention of liability. Defendant merely demonstrated that plaintiffs submitted solely objections in response to defendant's special interrogatories. However, even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, Mr. Shaiffer's deposition testimony and declaration and the Cleaver-Brooks drawings (plaintiffs' Exhibit D) would create a triable issue whether Mr. Shaiffer was exposed to an asbestos-containing product or material (gasket) attributable to defendant. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Cathy Meyer of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #11596 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

RULING:
DEFENDANT CRANE CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Crane Co.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication (negligent design defect, strict liability design defect, negligent failure to warn, strict liability failure to warn, and loss of consortium) are denied. As to the so-called OEM and asbestos content issues, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Shaiffer was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Mr. Shaiffer's deposition testimony and plaintiffs' responses to defendant's contention discovery preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. As to substantial factor causation, defendant sustained its burden and Dr. Bruch's declaration creates a triable issue whether Mr. Shaiffer's exposure to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his asbestos-related illness. As to loss of consortium, defendant sustained its burden and Mrs. Shaiffer's declaration creates a triable issue whether defendant is liable under such cause of action. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Mary Ann Scanlan, CSR # 8875 (503/TLJ)
